headline: same docs, every task, before any work
agents don't fail loud, they rot
sub: structural erosion up in 77% of runs. verbosity up in 75.5%.

headline: more context is not more help
sub: an agent reading 6 docs to answer one question pays for 5 it didn't need.

headline: same file. read 4 times. one session.
sub: nothing told it the file hadn't changed. once one store did: ~60% fewer tokens per lookup.

headline: i thought more docs meant a smarter agent
sub: wrong. less loaded per lookup, not more: ~60% fewer tokens.

headline: i can prove cheaper. not better.
secure by default is an assumption, not a setting
sub: 45% of generation tasks introduce an OWASP Top 10 flaw. only 55% came back secure.
source: Veracode, 2025 GenAI Code Security Report (150+ models)

headline: self-refinement is not self-improvement
sub: +37.6% critical vulnerabilities after 5 iterations of an agent fixing its own code.
source: Shukla, Joshi and Syed, arXiv:2506.11022 (2025), 400 samples

headline: what an honest ai pilot actually has
sub: an audit trail: which agent, reading what, when. ~60% fewer tokens per lookup, measured.
